examples.title: Basic JSON Schema editor
examples.description: Edit a flat object schema with form, JSON, and diff views.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { JsonSchemaEditor } from 'cinder/json-schema-editor';
  import type { JsonSchemaValue } from 'cinder/json-schema-editor';
  const personSchema: JsonSchemaValue = {
    $schema: 'https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema',
    title: 'Person',
    type: 'object',
    properties: {
      name: { type: 'string', title: 'Full name', minLength: 1 },
      age: { type: 'integer', minimum: 0 },
      email: { type: 'string', format: 'email' },
    },
    required: ['name'],
  };
</script>

<JsonSchemaEditor id="basic-jse" schema={personSchema} />

examples.title: Composition (oneOf)
examples.description: A schema using oneOf alongside type — both render as separate sections.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { JsonSchemaEditor } from 'cinder/json-schema-editor';
  import type { JsonSchemaValue } from 'cinder/json-schema-editor';
  const schema: JsonSchemaValue = {
    type: 'object',
    properties: {
      identifier: {
        oneOf: [
          { type: 'string', pattern: '^[a-z]+$' },
          { type: 'integer', minimum: 1 },
        ],
      },
    },
  };
</script>

<JsonSchemaEditor id="composition-jse" {schema} />

examples.title: Invalid initial schema
examples.description: When the input cannot be parsed, the form is disabled and the JSON view is the entry point.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { JsonSchemaEditor } from 'cinder/json-schema-editor';
  const malformed = '{ "type": "not-a-real-type" }';
</script>

<JsonSchemaEditor id="invalid-jse" schema={malformed} />
